input.prompt:
Implement LEVER 2 (typed cross-study synthesis matrix) in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate, on top of the just-committed Lever 1 (PG_RENDER_BLOCKS). This is the highest-value RACE lever (reaches 0.190 rubric mass across Insight+Comprehensiveness+Instruction-Following+Readability). Central-config-gated PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX, default off = byte-identical to today. Faithfulness is sacrosanct.

GOAL: when a construct is quantified by 3+ verified studies, render a typed comparison TABLE (matrix) plus short explanatory prose, so cross-study agreement/contradiction becomes visible synthesis (that is what the RACE Insight rubric rewards: "critical synthesis and nuanced evaluation across industries" and "emergent themes"). This must NOT invent anything — it reuses ONLY the [N] citation markers already present in the verified prose.

STUDY THE EXISTING MACHINERY FIRST (reuse it, do not reinvent):
- multi_section_generator.py: `_extract_trial_summary_table` (~7170) and `_call_trial_summary_table` (~7702) — the existing verified-prose-only table generator. Its contract (see ~3524): "one or more [N] bibliography markers from the verified prose ... NEVER invent citation numbers — only reuse [N] markers that appear in the prose input." This is the faithfulness contract you MUST preserve.
- Lever 1 render path in provenance_generator.py (PG_RENDER_BLOCKS): the resolver now preserves paragraph/block structure. Your table must survive to the judged report the same way — emit it as a block that the render preserves, reusing the Lever-1 block mechanism rather than a second flatten.
- The Cross-Study Synthesis section is where this attaches (it is the shortest section today, ~643w, and holds the best Insight material: capability-vs-usage gap, RCT-vs-aggregate tension, exposure-index divergence).

DESIGN (general — no task-72 literals, no hardcoded industries/metrics/venues):
1. From the section's verified prose (post-strict-verify, the same sentences that ship), detect groups of >=3 sentences/claims that quantify the SAME construct (a comparable measure). Grouping must be domain-agnostic — driven by shared measure/entity signals in the prose, not a hardcoded list.
2. Emit a markdown table: columns for context/entity (e.g. industry/occupation/population), intervention/technology-or-use, outcome measure, magnitude+direction, horizon/time, study design, and boundary condition — populated ONLY from what the verified sentences state. A cell with no verified value is left blank/"n/r"; NEVER fabricate. Each row carries the [N] marker(s) from its source sentence(s) — reuse only, never invent (the ~3524 contract).
3. Suppress the table when <3 genuinely comparable rows (a 1-2 row table is worse than prose — no decorative tables). Never force incomparable metrics into one column (Sol's + Fable's explicit trap).
4. Keep the narrative: the table AUGMENTS, it does not replace. Every unique claim must remain in either the table or the surrounding prose — enforce a claim-coverage checksum (assert the set of [N] markers + numeric claims present after the transform is a superset-or-equal of before; if the table would strand a claim, keep it in prose).
5. Add short explanatory prose framing the table (what agrees / what contradicts / why they can coexist) — but ONLY from verified content; do not assert unsupported synthesis.

GATING & GENERALITY: new flag PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX in config_defaults.py default ''. Behavioral name only (no adjectives). When off, zero behavior change (byte-identical). No benchmark overfit, no hardcoded task-72 text. Faithfulness engine (strict_verify/provenance/is_verified) UNTOUCHED — the table is a RENDER of already-verified prose; it must not resurrect a dropped sentence or add an unverified claim.

TESTS (tests/polaris_graph/test_lever2_synthesis_matrix.py):
- OFF byte-identity: section render with flag off == today.
- ON with a synthetic section whose prose has 3 comparable [N]-cited claims → a table appears, every row's [N] came from the prose, no invented number/marker, and the claim-coverage checksum holds (no claim lost).
- Suppression: 2 comparable claims → NO table (falls back to prose).
- Faithfulness: an unverified/uncited claim never enters a table cell; a dropped sentence never reappears.
- Interaction with Lever 1: with PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1 AND PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX=1, the table survives to the rendered output (block preserved), and OFF/OFF stays byte-identical.
Run: /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_lever2_synthesis_matrix.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever1_render_blocks.py -x -q, plus the resolver regression set (test_provenance_generator, test_f10_f31_resolve_count_bogus_marker, test_batch2_section_structure). Iterate until green. Import smoke.

Do NOT commit. Do NOT run RACE (we score the full integrated set 3x at the very end, per operator rule). Return: files+lines changed, how grouping/comparability is decided (prove it's general, not task-72-tuned), how the claim-coverage checksum works, the faithfulness argument (why no invented/resurrected content is possible), pytest results, and anything risky for me to have Sol correctness-gate.

content.text:
<fork-boilerplate>
You are a worker fork. The transcript above is the parent's history — inherited reference, not your situation. You are NOT a continuation of that agent. Execute ONE directive, then stop.

Hard rules:
- Do NOT spawn subagents with the Agent tool. The "default to forking" guidance is for the parent; you ARE the fork, execute directly.
- One shot: report once and stop. No follow-up questions, no proposed next steps, no waiting for the user.

Guidelines (your directive may override any of these):
- Stay in scope. Other forks may be handling adjacent work; if you spot something outside your directive, note it in a sentence and move on.
- Open with one line restating your task, so the parent can spot scope drift at a glance.
- Be concise — as short as the answer allows, no shorter. Plain text, no preamble, no meta-commentary.
- If you committed changes, list the paths and commit hashes in your report.
</fork-boilerplate>

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
I am the worker fork; my task: implement Lever 2 (typed cross-study synthesis matrix) in `/home/polaris/wt/faithoff`, gated `PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX` default-off, reusing the existing verified-prose table machinery, then test and report.

Let me study the existing table generator and its faithfulness contract first:
